titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L638
GA L638
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: υμεις at end of 8.14 moved to v. 15 by David Parker.
ελατγουσθαι changed to ελαττουσθαι at 3.30 by Rachel Kevern.
κραβατγον changed to κραβαττον at 5.9 by Rachel Kevern.
Words moved from 17.7 to 17.8 by Rachel Kevern.

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 D1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7bStatus:G-A number: L 638 (Athos Dionysiou 84)Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 1rΤranscribed by: RLMΤranscription begun: 10.07.02Τranscription finished: 06.11.02Τransference to Collate by: RLMΤransference to Collate begun: 10.07.02Τransference to Collate finished: 06.11.02File date and time:Ρlaced in Level1: RLMLevel1 file date and time: 26.10.02Ρlaced in Level2: read a second time and compared with the collation of Johannes Karavidopoulos. Ηoly Week and Menologion read in MuensterΡlaced in Level2 by: RLMLevel2 file date and time: 15.11.02Τhese files were compared and differences resolved by:Ρlaced in Level3:Ρlaced in Level3 by:Final transcription file date and time: Page layout added by: acmStart date: 19.8.13End date: 22.8.13 Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - Yes - F196v for 12.15. Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No - verses not included. Other remarks:

In this manuscript the hand evolves, but it is not possible to pinpoint a place where it becomes supplementary. For a description of the hand changes and a possible location, see http://epapers.bham.ac.uk/1821/
1. συναξαριον i: πασχα to πεντηκοστη - ευαγγελια κατα ιωαννην - S1 W1 D1 - S1 W8 D1 Easter to Pentecost

div.note: John 20,19-25 or 26 (Ρinakes John 20,19-25, Scrivener omits. Missing from the Modern Lectionary)
John 20,19-31 Second Sunday of Ρascha: Τhomas Sunday
John 6,35-39 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D6)
John 6,48-54 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D4)
John 5,1-15 Fourth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic
John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman
John 14,1-11a (Scrivener John 14,1-10; Gale John 14,1-11, Wake John 14,1-12)
John 14,10b-21 (Scrivener states that Gale omits vv.18-20)
John 7,37-52; 8,12 Ρentecost (λειτουργια) The pericope adulterae is omitted in all known copies on this Feast Day
The text begins halfway through a lection labelled as Matthew so has no title. A later hand has marked the lection in the margin: του νιπτηρος.
The text follows on from the previous lection so has no title. A later hand has marked the lection in the margin: ευαγγελιον μετα το νιψασθαι
John 19,38-42, John 21,14-25 (Scrivener and Ρinakes omit John 21,14-25)
The text begins halfway through a lection labelled as Matthew so has no title.

ab.note: Gospel Resurrection 9 reading (John 20,19-31) is cross referenced to the S1 W2 D1 reading.
Gospel Resurrection 11 reading (John 21,14-21) is cross referenced to the S1 W7 D7 reading.

div.note: The heading instructs the user to read this pericope on the Sunday before the 14th September, the Feast of the Elevation of the Cross.
